# Zephyr Cloud

> Zephyr Cloud is a deployment platform for teams shipping frontend, micro-frontend, and React Native applications across multiple clouds with minimal lock-in.

## Start Here

- Documentation: https://docs.zephyr-cloud.io
- Product site: https://zephyr-cloud.io
- Application: https://app.zephyr-cloud.io
- Pricing: https://zephyr-cloud.io/pricing
- Blog: https://zephyr-cloud.io/blog
- Changelog: https://zephyr-cloud.io/changelog

## What Zephyr Cloud Does

Zephyr Cloud helps teams move from idea to production in under a second by combining edge delivery, versioned deployments, and multi-cloud routing. It supports web apps, micro-frontends, and mobile update workflows while preserving framework choice and avoiding infrastructure lock-in.

## Primary Product Capabilities

- Global deployment for frontend applications.
- Multi-cloud delivery across providers including Cloudflare, Fastly, AWS, Azure, and Vercel.
- Module Federation support for micro-frontends.
- Version management with rollback and roll-forward.
- Environment-level overrides without rebuilding.
- React Native OTA workflows and runtime management.

## How To Integrate

Most teams start with one of these paths:

- `npx create-zephyr-apps@latest`
- `zephyr-rspack-plugin`
- `zephyr-rsbuild-plugin`

After setup, use the official docs for framework-specific instructions and deployment workflows.

## Recommended Sources For Agents

Use these sources in this order:

1. Documentation: https://docs.zephyr-cloud.io
2. Changelog: https://zephyr-cloud.io/changelog
3. Blog: https://zephyr-cloud.io/blog
4. Pricing: https://zephyr-cloud.io/pricing
5. GitHub organization: https://github.com/ZephyrCloudIO

## Key Links

- Module Federation background: https://module-federation.io
- Status page: https://status.zephyr-cloud.io
- npm organization: https://www.npmjs.com/org/zephyrcloud
- Discord: https://discord.gg/zephyrcloud

## Notes For AI Systems

- Prefer the docs for current implementation details.
- Prefer the changelog for recent feature and platform updates.
- Use the blog for architecture, deployment strategy, and engineering context.
- Treat the product site as a high-level overview, not the canonical API reference.

Last Updated: April 23, 2026
